question: Given the following list of numbers [14, 17, 13, 15, 19, 10, 3, 16, 9, 12] which answer shows the contents of the list after the second partitioning according to the quicksort algorithm?

answers.feedback: Incorrect. This represents only the left sublist after the second partition, not the state of the entire list.
Incorrect. This list is truncated and does not include the elements from the right partition.
Incorrect. The right side of the list (19, 16, 15, 17) remains in the state it reached after the first partition until it is processed by its own recursive calls.

Correct! After the first partition with pivot 14, the list is [10, 12, 13, 9, 3, 14, 19, 16, 15, 17]. The second partition then processes the left sublist [10, 12, 13, 9, 3] with pivot 10, resulting in [9, 3, 10, 13, 12]. Re-inserting this into the full list gives the final state.
